merged_prs_since_last_sync: #1 feat: structured access logs + Server-Timing header
#2 feat: split origin/cache buckets + canary mode (off/shadow/live)
#3 feat: include per-phase timings in JSON access log
#4 feat: extend worker trigger to multi-image / multi-format batches

resolved_questions.question: Should worker.go:78 hard-coded clientId=13 be removed?
resolved_questions.resolution: Resolved by PR #4 (extend-worker-payload). clientId now comes from BatchRequest.ClientID payload field. Regression-guard test asserts no Put key starts with '13/'.

resolved_questions.question: Should server.NewServer wire a destination S3 client to the internal worker?
resolved_questions.resolution: Resolved by PR #2 (split-origin-and-cache-buckets). When CACHE_MODE != off, cacheClient is wired as the worker's destS3Client. Dual-write becomes live for both server-path and worker-batch PUTs.

unresolved_questions: Should types.Storage interface be deleted (dead code) or wired up — is it the start of an aborted pluggable-storage refactor?

Should POST /_/worker/trigger gain a shared-secret header check, or is the LB-level authz contract documented elsewhere? (Risk now more concrete after PR #4 removed the clientId=13 limit on impersonation surface — see architecture.md §9 gap #5.)

graph_engine.reason: codebase-memory-mcp binary not present; tier-1 micro project allows direct read with High synthesis fidelity. Same as init run.

files_detected: README.md (now substantial: documents access log, CACHE_MODE, X-Use-Cache, worker trigger envelope)
context_audit.notes: README has grown into a legitimate operator-facing reference since init. Track spec.md files are authoritative for the 'why' of each merged PR; architecture.md is the synthesis on top.
